entities.name: MAIZE

entities.description: Pests causing weight and quality losses in stored maize

entities.description: The geographic area where the case study was conducted

entities.name: WEIGHT LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in mass of maize grain and cobs due to rodent activity

entities.description: The degradation of grain quality, including nutritional and safety aspects, due to rodent activity

entities.description: The technique used to identify rodent species and estimate their population

entities.description: The level of aflatoxin present in the grain, increased by rodent damage

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL VALUE
entities.description: The overall nutritional quality of the grain

entities.name: RATTUS RATTUS
entities.description: The specific rodent species captured during the study

entities.name: FUSARIUM SPP.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS SPP.
entities.description: A specific type of mould evaluated in the study

entities.name: DRY-MATTER

entities.name: CRUDE PROTEIN
entities.description: A nutritional component of the grain that decreases due to rodent damage

entities.name: FATTY ACID CONTENT
entities.description: A nutritional component of the grain that decreases due to rodent damage

entities.name: POSTHARVEST LOSSES
entities.description: The overall loss of crop quantity and quality after harvest

entities.description: Strategies to mitigate postharvest losses caused by rodents

entities.description: The environment where maize is stored and rodents cause damage
